tests.test_body:
define i8 @test(i1 %c, i64 %offset, ptr %ptr) {
; CHECK-LABEL: define i8 @test
; CHECK-SAME: (i1 [[C:%.*]], i64 [[OFFSET:%.*]], ptr [[PTR:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:  start:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[ALLOCA:%.*]] = alloca [8 x i8], align 8
; CHECK-NEXT:    store i64 1234605616436508552, ptr [[ALLOCA]], align 8
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[GEP_2:%.*]] = getelementptr i8, ptr [[ALLOCA]], i64 2
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[GEP_UNKNOWN:%.*]] = getelementptr i8, ptr [[ALLOCA]], i64 [[OFFSET]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[C]], label [[JOIN:%.*]], label [[IF:%.*]]
; CHECK:       if:
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label [[JOIN]]
; CHECK:       join:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[PHI:%.*]] = phi ptr [ [[GEP_UNKNOWN]], [[START:%.*]] ], [ [[GEP_2]], [[IF]] ]
; CHECK-NEXT:    store i8 0, ptr [[ALLOCA]], align 8
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[LOAD1:%.*]] = load i64, ptr [[ALLOCA]], align 8
; CHECK-NEXT:    store i64 [[LOAD1]], ptr [[PTR]], align 8
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[LOAD2:%.*]] = load i8, ptr [[PHI]], align 1
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i8 [[LOAD2]]
start:
  %alloca = alloca [8 x i8], align 8
  store i64 1234605616436508552, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %gep.2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 2
  %gep.unknown = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 %offset
  br i1 %c, label %join, label %if

if:                                               ; preds = %start
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if, %start
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep.unknown, %start ], [ %gep.2, %if ]
  store i8 0, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %load1 = load i64, ptr %alloca, align 8
  store i64 %load1, ptr %ptr, align 8
  %load2 = load i8, ptr %phi, align 1
  ret i8 %load2
}
tests.source_program:
define i8 @test(i1 %c, i64 %offset, ptr %ptr) {
start:
  %alloca = alloca [8 x i8], align 8
  store i64 1234605616436508552, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %gep.2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 2
  %gep.unknown = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 %offset
  br i1 %c, label %join, label %if

if:                                               ; preds = %start
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if, %start
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep.unknown, %start ], [ %gep.2, %if ]
  store i8 0, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %load1 = load i64, ptr %alloca, align 8
  store i64 %load1, ptr %ptr, align 8
  %load2 = load i8, ptr %phi, align 1
  ret i8 %load2
}

issue.title: Invalid optimization around loops
issue.author: Noratrieb

comments.author: efriedma-quic
comments.body: You might want to try using opt-bisect-limit (https://llvm.org/docs/OptBisect.html) to reduce this.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
Reduced:
```llvm
; RUN: opt -S -passes=gvn < %s
define i8 @src(i64 %offset, ptr %ptr) {
start:
  %alloca = alloca [8 x i8], align 8
  store i64 u0x1122334455667788, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %gep.2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 2
  %gep.unknown = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 %offset
  br label %bb

unreachable:
  br label %bb

bb:
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep.unknown, %start ], [ %gep.2, %unreachable ]
  store i8 0, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %load1 = load i64, ptr %alloca, align 8
  store i64 %load1, ptr %ptr, align 8
  %load2 = load i8, ptr %phi, align 1
  ret i8 %load2
}
```
Produces:
```llvm
define i8 @src(i64 %offset, ptr %ptr) {
start:
  %alloca = alloca [8 x i8], align 8
  store i64 1234605616436508552, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %gep.2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 2
  %gep.unknown = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 %offset
  br label %bb

unreachable:                                      ; No predecessors!
  br label %bb

bb:                                               ; preds = %unreachable, %start
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep.unknown, %start ], [ %gep.2, %unreachable ]
  store i8 0, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %load1 = load i64, ptr %alloca, align 8
  store i64 %load1, ptr %ptr, align 8
  %0 = lshr i64 %load1, 16
  %1 = trunc i64 %0 to i8
  ret i8 %1
}
```
Note how the extract is at offset 2 rather than offset `%offset`.

Edit: This also works without the unreachable predecessor. This is a slightly more natural test case:
```llvm
define i8 @src(i1 %c, i64 %offset, ptr %ptr) {
start:
  %alloca = alloca [8 x i8], align 8
  store i64 u0x1122334455667788, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %gep.2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 2
  %gep.unknown = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 %offset
  br i1 %c, label %join, label %if

if:
  br label %join

join:
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep.unknown, %start ], [ %gep.2, %if ]
  store i8 0, ptr %alloca, align 8
  %load1 = load i64, ptr %alloca, align 8
  store i64 %load1, ptr %ptr, align 8
  %load2 = load i8, ptr %phi, align 1
  ret i8 %load2
}
```

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
The AA trace contains this:
```
  End   %gep.unknown = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 %offset @ LocationSize::precise(1),   %alloca = alloca [8 x i8], align 8 @ LocationSize::precise(8) = PartialAlias
[...]
  End   %gep.2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %alloca, i64 2 @ LocationSize::precise(1),   %alloca = alloca [8 x i8], align 8 @ LocationSize::precise(8) = PartialAlias (off -2)
End   %alloca = alloca [8 x i8], align 8 @ LocationSize::precise(8),   %phi = phi ptr [ %gep.unknown, %start ], [ %gep.2, %if ] @ LocationSize::precise(1) = PartialAlias (off 2)
```
So this is probably due to incorrect merging of PartialAlias with an without offsets.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
Fixes a pretty serious miscompile, so we may consider this for backport, though the timing is a bit unfortunate.

/cherry-pick 4d64ffa94170eadd79954e2a5f13d1f1d16e9e2c 97f0e7b06e6b76fd85fb81b8c12eba2255ff1742
